title: Quality metrics dataset (acceptance, alignment error, pronunciation rate, consent/rights completeness)
context: After batch-001 and batch-002 produce measurable output, the project needs a structured dataset of quality metrics — acceptance rate, median alignment error, pronunciation error rate, audio-quality conformance, and consent/rights completeness per chapter/work — to track pipeline health over time and inform the outcome-tracking dashboard (pipeline-003). Without a stable metrics format, runs are not comparable and the project cannot demonstrate whether quality is improving or degrading. Depends on batch-001 (first run producing measurable data).
objective: Define and populate a quality-metrics dataset schema covering all key pipeline metrics per chapter and per run, populated from the batch-001 and batch-002 run reports.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines a quality-metrics schema with fields: work/chapter id, run id, date, acceptance rate, median sentence/word onset error (ms), pronunciation error rate (per 1,000 words), audio-quality conformance rate (%), fidelity defect count, consent completeness (%), rights completeness (%), and synthetic-label completeness (%).
Populates the dataset from the batch-001 and batch-002 run reports with at least one entry per batch run.
Schema and dataset are published under CC0-1.0.
The dataset format is machine-readable (JSON or CSV with a schema file) and suitable for ingestion by a future outcome-tracking dashboard (pipeline-003).
resources: read-aloud-audio-batch-001 (run report with acceptance rate, alignment error)
PLAN.md (success metrics table, M2 exit criteria)
output: A quality-metrics dataset (metrics/quality-metrics.json or .csv) and its schema definition (metrics/quality-metrics.schema.json), CC0-1.0, populated with run data from batch-001 and batch-002.
